Caption: (a) The system of extended volume accelerating rate calorimeter (ARC); (b) The test vessel with specially tailored fixture for the tested prismatic battery cells; (c) Flow chart of ARC testing protocol; (d) Schematic of thermal runaway propagation (TRP) test setup in the three-cell stack.

Overview of SEI growth and electrolyte retention during battery aging; data taken from literature [20,25,48,50–54]. The solid dots indicate experimental results, while the open circles represent estimations derived from the simulation studies. The shaded area represents a confidence interval of 95%. Extrapolation to different cell designs and operating conditions should be considered with caution unless supported by additional data.

(a) Temperature profiles of single-cell simulation and ARC test; (b) Temperature increase rate of single-cell simulation and ARC test; (c) Temperature profiles of Cell no. 1, Cell no. 2, and Cell no. 3 during thermal runaway propagation.

(a) Thermal runaway temperature profile of aged battery cells; (b) Heat sources of these aged cells from different reactions and from an internal short circuit; (c) Temperature profiles of Cell no. 1, Cell no. 2, and Cell no. 3 during thermal runaway propagation. The solid curves represent fresh battery cells with 100% SOH. The dashed and dotted curves represent aged cells with 80% and 50% SOH, respectively.

Time of TR onset from Cell no. 1 to Cell no. 3 during TRP across various SOH levels. (a-1) Fast SEI growth with slow electrolyte decomposition; (a-2) Fast SEI growth with moderate electrolyte decomposition; (a-3) Fast SEI growth with fast electrolyte decomposition; (b-1) Moderate SEI growth with slow electrolyte decomposition; (b-2) Moderate SEI growth with moderate electrolyte decomposition; (b-3) Moderate SEI growth with fast electrolyte decomposition; (c-1) Slow SEI growth with slow electrolyte decomposition; (c-2) Slow SEI growth with moderate electrolyte decomposition; (c-3) Slow SEI growth with fast electrolyte decomposition.
